findings.issue: Task 5 'Files to read' references `core-runtime/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/data/DefaultDownloadRepository.kt`. This file does not exist. The class `DefaultDownloadRepository` lives in `DownloadRepository.kt` — the same file contains both the interface and the implementation class. The companion field `mainActivityFqn` is at `DownloadRepository.kt:81`.
findings.fix: Replace the reference with `core-runtime/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/data/DownloadRepository.kt` in the 'Code files — read for context' section of Task 5.

findings.issue: Task 6 implementation hint uses `::System.nanoTime` as a method reference. In Kotlin, `System.nanoTime` is a Java static method and cannot be referenced with `::` on the object; it must be either a lambda `{ System.nanoTime() }` or the companion-object reference form. The correct idiomatic Kotlin is `{ System.nanoTime() }`, not `::System.nanoTime`. The hint says `nowNanos = ::System.nanoTime` which will not compile.
findings.fix: Change the hint to `nowNanos = { System.nanoTime() }` in Task 6 Details / implementation hints section.

findings.issue: Task 6 'What to do' step 5 and 'Details/imports' block suggests `java.time.LocalDateTime` and `java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter` for the suggested filename timestamp. The project's own production code (app/src/) does not use `java.time` anywhere — it is not a pattern established in this codebase. Task 4 uses `SimpleDateFormat("yyyyMMdd-HHmm", Locale.ROOT)` for the identical format string. Using two different APIs for the same formatting purpose across tasks 4 and 6 is inconsistent. `java.time` is available at minSdk=31 without desugaring, so there is no compilation risk — but the inconsistency with the established project pattern and with Task 4 is an unnecessary divergence.
findings.fix: Replace `java.time.LocalDateTime.now().format(DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyyMMdd-HHmm"))` with `SimpleDateFormat("yyyyMMdd-HHmm", Locale.ROOT).format(Date())` to match the pattern used in Task 4 and the existing codebase. Update the imports list accordingly (`java.text.SimpleDateFormat`, `java.util.Date`, `java.util.Locale` instead of `java.time.*`).
